commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to all five Anthropic `createClient` SDK constructor branches and adds constructor-wiring plus real-SDK/fetch-stub tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +130. Total +136 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current mainâ€™s Anthropic `createClient` branches do not pass the SDK `fetch` hook, and the runtime path uses that client for `messages.create(...).asResponse()`. I did not run a live oversized-response reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of guarded-transport compatibility risk, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(anthropic): wire buildGuardedModelFetch into all 5 createClient SDK constructors
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `createClient()` function in `anthropic.ts` creates 5 `new Anthropic({...})` SDK instances â€” one for each auth mode (API key, Cloudflare AI Gateway, GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, OAuth). None of them pass `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)`, so all 5 are missing the 16 MiB bounded-read SSRF guard that the transport layer (`anthropic-transport-stream.ts:801-802`) already uses.

Without the guard, a hostile or broken Anthropic API endpoint (or any man-in-the-middle proxy / enterprise gateway between OpenClaw and `api.anthropic.com`) can push an unbounded response body and force the runtime to buffer it entirely before the caller's bounded reader gets a chance to cap.

## Why This Change Was Made

`buildGuardedModelFetch` is the canonical fetch wrapper for provider SDK constructors â€” it applies SSRF protection, timeout, retry, and a 16 MiB response body cap. Every other SDK constructor in the codebase already uses it:

- `openai-transport-stream.ts` (3 `new OpenAI({...})` branches, lines 2022/2611/2676)
- `openai-completions.ts` (line 620)
- `openai-responses.ts` (line 176)
- `azure-openai-responses.ts` (line 202)
- `anthropic-transport-stream.ts` (2 `new Anthropic({...})` branches, lines 801-802)

The Anthropic SDK accepts `fetch?: Fetch | undefined` per its `client.ts:379` constructor options. This PR brings the LLM provider's `createClient()` in line with the transport layer and all other SDK constructors.

## Evidence

### Layer 1: Mock-based constructor wiring (5 branches)

`anthropic.test.ts` adds a table-driven test (`it.each`) that verifies all 5 `createClient` branches receive `buildGuardedModelFetch` in the SDK constructor options:

Each test case:
1. Configures a `model` + `apiKey` that targets one of the 5 branches (API key, Cloudflare, Copilot, Foundry, OAuth)
2. Calls `streamAnthropic(model, context, { apiKey })`
3. Asserts `typeof config.fetch === "function"` on the captured SDK constructor options

```
 âś“ createClient fetch injection (5 branches) > wires buildGuardedModelFetch into default API key auth constructor
 âś“ createClient fetch injection (5 branches) > wires buildGuardedModelFetch into Cloudflare AI Gateway constructor
 âś“ createClient fetch injection (5 branches) > wires buildGuardedModelFetch into GitHub Copilot constructor
 âś“ createClient fetch injection (5 branches) > wires buildGuardedModelFetch into Microsoft Foundry bearer auth constructor
 âś“ createClient fetch injection (5 branches) > wires buildGuardedModelFetch into OAuth token constructor
```

### Layer 2: Real HTTP pipeline interception

`anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts` proves the custom fetch is actually used for HTTP â€” not just stored in constructor options:

1. Stubs `globalThis.fetch` with `vi.stubGlobal("fetch", vi.fn(...))` â€” **no mock of `@anthropic-ai/sdk`**
2. The real SDK receives `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as its `fetch` option
3. When the SDK issues `POST /v1/messages`, it calls `this.fetch(url, init)` â†’ `buildGuardedModelFetch` â†’ `globalThis.fetch` (our spy)
4. Returns 500 to short-circuit; SDK retries 2-3x on 500

**Captured call:**
```
URL:       https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages
Method:    POST
Body:      { model: "claude-sonnet-4-6", stream: true, messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }] }
```

### Test results

```
âś“ 42 tests passed (2 files)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts           â€” 41 tests (+5 new)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts â€” 1 test (new)
```

### Diff scope

```
3 files changed, 136 insertions(+)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts                  |  6 +++   (1 import + 5 fetch: lines)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts             | 46 +++++++ (5 table-driven branch tests)
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts | 84 ++++++++ (real HTTP pipeline proof)
```

### Verification commands

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts
# 42 passed âś“

npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts \
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.fetch-proof.test.ts
# No new errors âś“

pnpm tsgo:core  # exit 0 âś“
```

### What was not tested

- The `anthropic-transport-stream.ts` path already has `buildGuardedModelFetch` (lines 801-802); not changed by this PR.
- No loopback HTTP server test â€” covered by the fetch-interception pattern used in #97628 (proven đź¦ž in single re-review).
- The 16 MiB cap behavior is exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`; this PR only wires the guard into the constructor.
